pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 23, export_result.sh line 14, verifier.py line 66 all use identical path
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 24, export_result.sh line 15, verifier.py line 85 all use identical path

Task description, export_result.sh line 42 checks window titles for 'Pulse|Oximeter|SpO2', verifier.py checks pulse_ox_running flag. OpenICE documentation confirms pulse oximeter device simulation support.

Verifier logic in verifier.py lines 110-117: awards full cropping points when asset_area < (full_area * 0.4), meaning assets should be 40% or less of original area. Comment on line 108 states 'e.g., < 25% area'. Metadata claims 0.75 (75% of original) contradicts this requirement.
Verifier logic in verifier.py lines 134-137: awards aspect ratio points when aspect > 1.5. Metadata claim matches code requirement exactly.

privileged_info_summary: Task uses OpenICE, a medical device simulator, to generate synthetic pulse oximeter waveform data. The task requires creating a cropped, grayscale image asset of a plethysmograph waveform. Verified configuration: output paths are /home/ga/Desktop/full_screen_capture.png and /home/ga/Desktop/fig_01_pleth_waveform.png; required device is Pulse Oximeter; valid aspect ratio is >1.5. Critical discrepancy: metadata claims min_crop_ratio of 0.75 (preserving 75% of original area), but verifier code requires assets to be <40% of original area for full points, contradicting the metadata claim.
